other_observers: Brandon Percival, Duane Nelson, Roger Linfield, Steve Larson, Sherrie Chapman and others.; Originally found by Brandon Percival, identification confirmed by Bill Maynard and many people on the following day, Saturday.
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch, which is about 10 miles east of I25 on the county line between El Paso and Pueblo.
description: A Brown Thrasher was seen very nearby along with this bird. The size and structure of the two birds was essentially the same. There were four main differences between the birds. First, the color of the top of the head, entire back and upper tail were a quite dowdy shade of brown, as opposed to the much richer brown/rufous of the BT. Second, the entire face and back onto the foreneck was a dark gray, as opposed to a much lighter shade of brown to gray in the BT. Third, the bill was entirely black with no lighter color on the mandible, and looked just a touch longer than the BT bill. Fourth, the breast streaking, especially on the sides, was black and bold on a white background, as opposed to less bold on an off-white background in the BT. The Long-billed Thrasher seemed to have an orangier eye, but this is a pretty subjective fieldmark. Finally, both birds had two white wingbars and darkish legs.Behaviors: The bird would feed and hide very low to the ground in thick tangles especially when people came close. However, a few minutes later it would appear higher in the Russian Olives and sometimes eat their friut. This behavior is typical of the Thrasher family.Call: None heardPlumage: Winter
